settings.title: Monster Piercing | Captive Bead Rings
settings.text:
Discover the timeless appeal of our Captive Bead Rings Collection, featuring beautifully crafted pieces that combine secure design, versatility, and effortless style. Each ring includes a removable bead or gem held perfectly in place, offering a sleek and comfortable fit for a wide range of piercings.

Available in titanium, surgical steel, silver, and gold finishes, these rings are designed for durability and long-lasting shine. Whether you prefer a simple polished bead, a gemstone accent, or a minimalist finish, our captive bead rings are the perfect choice for ears, noses, lips, or cartilage piercings.

This collection blends classic body jewellery design with modern craftsmanship, creating pieces that feel as good as they look. Designed for everyday wear or special occasions, they’re a must-have for anyone who loves dainty, versatile body jewellery with a clean, elegant look.

settings.question: What are captive bead rings?
settings.answer: Captive bead rings, also known as CBRs or ball closure rings, are circular pieces of body jewellery that use a small removable bead or ball to close the ring securely. The tension of the ring holds the bead in place, creating a seamless, secure look.

settings.question: What’s the difference between clicker rings and standard captive bead rings?
settings.answer:
• Clicker rings feature a hinge and clasp mechanism that “clicks” into place. They are easy to open and close without tools, making them ideal for frequent wear or jewellery changes.
  • Standard captive bead rings use a small bead that fits tightly between the ends of the ring and is held in place by pressure. They’re more traditional and often preferred for their simplicity and secure fit

settings.question: How do I insert or remove a captive bead ring?
settings.answer:
• For clicker rings,
    simply open the hinged section, insert the ring into your piercing, and click it shut until you hear it snap into place.
  • For standard captive bead rings, gently separate the ring ends just enough to release or replace the bead. Ring-opening pliers can be used for thicker gauges to make the process easier.

settings.question: What piercings can captive bead rings be used for?
settings.answer: Captive bead rings and clickers can be used in many piercings including nose, ear, lip, septum, eyebrow, daith, rook, conch, tragus, and nipple piercings. The versatility of the design makes them one of the most adaptable jewellery types available.
